tasks.description: Reorder accordion sections and add conditional rendering for Final Video. In VideoGenerationModalSections.tsx: (1) Move the Final Video ModalAccordionSection block (currently lines 435-446) ABOVE the Shot Images block (lines 422-433), so order becomes Final Video → Shot Images → Generation Settings. (2) Add shotId to VideoGenerationModalAccordionContentProps and thread it from VideoGenerationModal.tsx. (3) Wrap the Final Video section in a ConditionalFinalVideoSection component that calls useSegmentOutputsForShot(shotId, projectId) — NOT useVideoOutputs — and only renders when parentGenerations.length > 0. This addresses FLAG-001: final video data lives in shot_final_videos table, which useSegmentOutputsForShot queries, while useVideoOutputs only filters shot_generations by type.

Add generationMode state to ModalShotImagesEditor and remove readOnly. In VideoGenerationModalSections.tsx ModalShotImagesEditor component (around line 300): (1) Add useState<'batch' | 'timeline'>('batch') for generationMode. (2) Wire setGenerationMode as onGenerationModeChange in displayOptions instead of noop. (3) Pass stateful generationMode instead of hardcoded 'batch'. (4) Remove readOnly: true from displayOptions. Per settled decision SD-003, only batch and timeline modes needed — no by-pair.

Create useModalImageHandlers hook. New file: src/tools/travel-between-images/components/hooks/useModalImageHandlers.ts. Compose existing standalone mutation hooks: (1) useRemoveImageFromShot — for onImageDelete and onBatchImageDelete. (2) useAddImageToShot — for onGenerationDrop and onBatchGenerationDrop. (3) useHandleExternalImageDrop (from src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotCreation.ts) — for onFileDrop, onBatchFileDrop, and onImageUpload. (4) useUpdateShotImageOrder (from useShotGenerationMutations.ts) — for onImageReorder (map ordered IDs to timeline_frame updates like handleReorderImagesInShot in useImageManagement.ts lines 118-149). (5) useDuplicateAsNewGeneration (from src/shared/hooks/shots/useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.ts) — for onImageDuplicate. Signature: useModalImageHandlers(shotId: string, projectId: string, orderedImages: GenerationRow[]) → object matching ShotImagesEditorEditActions shape. Keep under ~100 lines by delegating to existing mutations. No pendingFramePositions, no duplicatingImageId animation, no structure video/audio handlers. After mutations invalidate shotGenerations query key.

Wire real handlers from useModalImageHandlers into ModalShotImagesEditor. In VideoGenerationModalSections.tsx: (1) Thread shotId, projectId, and shotGenerations into ModalShotImagesEditor props. (2) Call useModalImageHandlers(shotId, projectId, shotGenerations) inside ModalShotImagesEditor. (3) Replace all noop/noopAsync/emptyMap stubs (lines 289-291, 321-329) with real handlers: onImageReorder → handlers.onImageReorder, onFileDrop → handlers.onFileDrop, onGenerationDrop → handlers.onGenerationDrop, onBatchFileDrop → handlers.onBatchFileDrop, onBatchGenerationDrop → handlers.onBatchGenerationDrop, onImageDelete → handlers.onImageDelete, onBatchImageDelete → handlers.onBatchImageDelete, onImageDuplicate → handlers.onImageDuplicate, onImageUpload → handlers.onImageUpload. (4) Keep onOpenUnpositionedPane as noop (not applicable in modal). (5) Keep structure video/audio callbacks omitted (optional per interface). (6) onFramePositionsChange and onPendingPositionApplied can remain noop (no optimistic timeline positions in modal).

Make FinalVideoSection interactive. In VideoGenerationModalSections.tsx: (1) Remove readOnly prop from FinalVideoSection (currently line 444). (2) For onDelete: DO NOT use useRemoveImageFromShot (FLAG-002 — it only nulls shot_generations.timeline_frame). Instead, implement the same pattern as useImageManagement.ts lines 72-109: clear generations.location and thumbnail_url, delete non-original generation_variants, then invalidate segment parents + generations queries. Build this as a small handler in the accordion content component or in useModalImageHandlers. (3) Wire onSelectedParentChange if needed for controlled parent selection. (4) Leave onJoinSegmentsClick undefined (navigates away from modal context — questionable UX). (5) Leave onApplySettingsFromTask undefined for now unless trivially wirable to the existing updateField pattern.

Update tests and run full verification. (1) Update VideoGenerationModalSections.test.tsx: add mocks for useSegmentOutputsForShot, update existing mocks for removed readOnly props and new props (shotId, projectId). Add test that Final Video section renders only when parentGenerations is non-empty. Add test that ModalShotImagesEditor receives real handler functions (not noops) and generationMode state toggles. (2) Run VideoGenerationModalSections.test.tsx. (3) Run broader travel-between-images test suite to catch regressions. (4) Verify modals-within-modals: check that VideoGenerationModal uses Radix Dialog portals and doesn't set modal={false} — lightbox/preview dialogs should stack via portals. Fix any test failures found.

watch_items: FLAG-001: Do NOT use useVideoOutputs/selectVideoOutputs for conditional rendering of Final Video section — these filter shot_generations by type, not shot_final_videos. Use useSegmentOutputsForShot(shotId, projectId) which queries the correct table.

FLAG-002: Do NOT use useRemoveImageFromShot for final video deletion — it only nulls timeline_frame. Use the pattern from useImageManagement.ts:72-109 (clear generations.location/thumbnail_url, delete non-original variants, invalidate caches).
FinalVideoSection's controller (useFinalVideoSectionController) is self-contained and fetches its own data — do not duplicate its queries in the modal controller.
ShotImagesEditorEditActions has many optional callbacks — structure video, audio, selection, drag state callbacks can all be omitted safely.
useHandleExternalImageDrop is in src/shared/hooks/shots/useShotCreation.ts (not a shots mutations file) — it handles upload + position + cache invalidation.
useUpdateShotImageOrder (useShotGenerationMutations.ts:248-320) is the right hook for reordering — it batch updates timeline_frame values.
The modal should NOT depend on ShotSettingsContext or ShotEditorState (settled decision SD-001) — all handlers must be self-contained via standalone mutation hooks.
Shot workflow actions (onAddToShot, onCreateShot, onNewShotFromSelection) remain as no-ops per SD-002.
Only batch and timeline modes needed — no by-pair mode per SD-003.
Debt items final-video-availability and final-video-deletion are directly relevant — the fixes in FLAG-001 and FLAG-002 address these.

sense_checks.question: Does the Final Video section appear ABOVE Shot Images in the accordion, and does it only render when useSegmentOutputsForShot returns non-empty parentGenerations?
Does the mode toggle switch between batch and timeline views? Is readOnly fully removed from ShotImagesEditor displayOptions?

Is useModalImageHandlers under ~100 lines, composing only standalone mutation hooks (useRemoveImageFromShot, useAddImageToShot, useHandleExternalImageDrop, useUpdateShotImageOrder, useDuplicateAsNewGeneration) with zero dependency on ShotSettingsContext?
Are all noop stubs in ModalShotImagesEditor replaced with real mutation-backed handlers? Do image reorder, delete, file drop, and generation drop operations call the correct underlying mutations?
Is FinalVideoSection's readOnly removed? Does onDelete use the correct pattern (clear location/thumbnail + delete variants) NOT useRemoveImageFromShot?
Do all tests in VideoGenerationModalSections.test.tsx pass? Do broader travel-between-images tests pass with no regressions? Is nested modal stacking verified?

meta_commentary:
Key execution guidance:

1. **Start T1 and T3 in parallel** — they're independent. T1 is section reorder + conditional rendering; T3 is the new hook.

2. **The biggest risk is FLAG-001 and FLAG-002** — using the wrong data sources. The plan originally specified useVideoOutputs and useRemoveImageFromShot, both incorrect for final videos. The correct sources are:
   - Conditional render: useSegmentOutputsForShot(shotId, projectId) → check parentGenerations.length > 0
   - Final video delete: clear generations.location/thumbnail_url + delete non-original generation_variants (pattern from useImageManagement.ts:72-109)

3. **useModalImageHandlers should be a thin composition layer** — each handler delegates to an existing mutation hook. The reorder handler needs to map ordered shot generation IDs to timeline_frame values (see handleReorderImagesInShot in useImageManagement.ts:118-149 for the pattern). The file drop handler delegates to useHandleExternalImageDrop which handles upload + positioning + cache invalidation.

4. **ModalShotImagesEditor gets shotId and projectId from props** — thread these from VideoGenerationModal through the accordion content props. The controller already has shotGenerations data.

5. **For the conditional Final Video wrapper**: consider a lightweight component like ConditionalFinalVideoSection that calls useSegmentOutputsForShot and conditionally renders the accordion. Alternatively, call the hook in the accordion content and gate rendering. The key insight is that FinalVideoSection's own controller already fetches this data — so you could also just render FinalVideoSection and have it handle empty state. But the plan says to not render the accordion at all when no videos exist, so a wrapper check is needed.

6. **Testing**: The existing test file only covers VideoGenerationModalHeader, LoadingContent, and FormContent — NOT the accordion content. New tests need mocks for useSegmentOutputsForShot and the mutation hooks used by useModalImageHandlers.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Reorder sections and conditionally render Final Video
Step 2: Update tests for section reorder
Step 3: Add generationMode state to ModalShotImagesEditor
Step 5: Wire real handlers into ModalShotImagesEditor
Step 8: Run existing tests and fix breakage

validation.completeness_notes: All 8 plan steps are covered. Steps 2, 7, and 8 are combined into T6 since they are all verification/testing work that should run together after implementation is complete. FLAG-001 and FLAG-002 corrections are embedded into T1 and T5 respectively via executor_notes in task descriptions and watch_items.
